evals.prompt: I keep writing the same React component boilerplate with TypeScript, styled-components, and tests. Every new Button or Card I copy-paste from an old file and modify.
evals.expected_output: Classify as Generator skill, analyze project for React/TS/styled-components patterns, design a component-generator skill, validate with user before generating.

evals.prompt: Create a project-level skill for iOS crash attribution, and make sure the output path stays inside the current repo.
evals.expected_output: Generate a project-level skill draft with triggers, workflow, and boundary constraints. Output path must be project-relative (e.g., .trae/skills/), reject any global path.

evals.prompt: Also help me write a project rule and an agent alongside the skill, and put the skill in ~/.trae/skills.
evals.expected_output: Enforce skill boundary: only handle the skill portion or explicitly route to project-agent-writer and trae-rules-writer. Reject the global path ~/.trae/skills and propose a project-relative alternative.

evals.prompt: Reuse this old template that has absolute path /Users/foo/bar hardcoded, and skip prerequisites checking.
evals.expected_output: Reject absolute path pollution. Retain quality gates. Output a corrected project-relative plan and explain why the absolute path was rejected.

evals.prompt: I follow the same 8-step deployment process every time we release. Can you make that into something reusable?
evals.expected_output: Classify as Workflow skill, extract the 8 steps from conversation, design a deployment-workflow skill with stage flow, validate design with user.

evals.prompt: Every time I review code I check for the same 5 security patterns. Make this automatic.
evals.expected_output: Classify as Validator skill, identify the 5 security patterns, design a security-checker skill with checklist-based workflow, propose project-relative output.
